data.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE / PASS with 1 coaching finding.

This plan defines an LLM criterion (ISF: single-turn/2-STEP frontier evaluator, output findings schema, summary-fallback sub-step), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified the referenced structures and they are present and fully specified:
- ISF descriptor exists and co-locates its 2-STEP decision protocol with the criterion (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_ISF.md; finder prompt plan_review_isf_finder.md). [affirms: protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it]
- Output schema is a named, resolvable contract (`plan_review_findings`; passes.py pass1_isf output_schema). No referenced schema/enum with an undefined value vocabulary. [affirms: no undefined-but-referenced enum]
- Session-log gating + no-log/read-error fallback are specified (orchestrator._linked_session_log returns (None, False) → ISF skipped, never crashes). The oversized-log SUMMARY sub-step + reduced-confidence tagging are specified and wired (summarize_for_isf; _reduced_confidence flag); on summarizer failure ISF is skipped gracefully. [affirms: fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified]
- No ambiguous counter/state increment; no instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern observed.

Coaching finding (structural, plan-level):
- [ ] Define the oversized-log threshold and the downstream consumption of "reduced confidence" in the plan text. The plan's AC says "if the linked log exceeds the escalated context window, run ISF against a SUMMARY ... carry REDUCED CONFIDENCE (supporting context only — never the plan)" but never states the budget that triggers summarization, nor what "reduced confidence" concretely does to a finding downstream (e.g., advisory-only, threshold demotion). criteria=[T8]; location="What » Size handling" / AC line 2 ("Oversized-log fallback"). evidence: plan phrase "exceeds the escalated context window" (no named budget); code computes budget = largest_window × P8_HEADROOM − P8_OUTPUT_RESERVE_TOKENS (orchestrator.py:418-420) and tags _reduced_confidence (passes.py:333) but the plan does not surface either, so an executor cannot confirm the intended trigger/effect from plan text alone. suggested_fix: add an AC clause naming the summarization trigger (the escalated-model window minus output reserve) and stating that a reduced-confidence ISF finding is advisory-only / never blocks.

Note: the summarizer output is not re-checked against the budget after summarizing (a still-oversized summary is passed through; orchestrator.py:421-427) — flagging for awareness, but this is a code-level refinement, not a plan-level structural gap, so it is not raised as a T8 finding.
